other_observers: David Dowell and Larry Arnold, who agreed with ID;
duration: seen a couple times during visit to Beecher. Total time of observation 4-5 minutes
specific_location: Beecher Island, just e. of bridge over creek.
description: Brightly and contrastingly colored "Solitary Vireo." Deep blue-gray head contrasting sharply with snowy white throat. Moderately bright yellow sides without any infusion of olive. Back bright green (similar to that of TOWA in color) with moderately sharp demarcation with blue-gray nape. Relatively broad white outer tail edges. Secondaries brightly edged in yellow-green. Tertials with bright white tips. Spectacle quite contrasting and white. Wingbars bold and white.Behaviors: Moved slowly through woodland, mostly at mid-level in tall thicketsCall: not heardPlumage: unknown
